python-internals.title: Global interpreter lock - Wikipedia
python-internals.description: A global interpreter lock (GIL) is a mutual-exclusion lock held by a programming language interpreter thread to avoid sharing code that is not thread-safe with other threads. In implementations with a GIL, there is always one GIL for each interpreter process.

python-internals.title: Inside Python: How your code runs, from source to execution | Medium
python-internals.description: Your code goes through parsing, compiling, bytecode generation, executing inside a virtual machine, interacting with memory managers, and respecting the GIL.

python-internals.title: GlobalInterpreterLock - Python Wiki
python-internals.description: In CPython, the global interpreter lock, or GIL, is a mutex that protects access to Python objects, preventing multiple threads from executing Python bytecodes at once.

python-internals.title: What Is the Python Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)? – Real Python
python-internals.description: Python's Global Interpreter Lock or GIL, in simple words, is a mutex that allows only one thread to hold the control of the Python interpreter at any one time.

python-internals.title: CPython Internals & Performance: The Ultimate Guide to the Python...
python-internals.description: How Python transforms your code into bytecode, why the GIL exists and what it really does behind the scenes, how memory management works.

python-internals.title: How Your Python Code Actually Runs
python-internals.description: Python's memory allocator (obmalloc) manages the PyLongObject allocation from its pool — no syscall needed for small objects. The eval loop advances the instruction pointer.

python-internals.title: How to Implement Basic Multiprocessing with While Loops in Python
python-internals.description: The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is a mutex in Python that prevents multiple native threads from executing Python bytecode simultaneously.

python-internals.title: CPython-Internals memory_management.md - GitHub
python-internals.description: CPython has implemented its own memory management mechanism. When you create a new object in a Python program, it's not directly malloced from the heap.
